question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: execute()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted `ContractDebugParameters` with arbitrary `ethereumData` bytes to force `buildEthereumTransactionBody()` to replay a historical Ethereum transaction with a completely different payload, causing the mirror node to produce a falsified execution trace that diverges from the actual Hashgraph-recorded history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: patchSenderNonce()] Can an unprivileged external user craft `ethereumData` with an arbitrary nonce value so that `patchSenderNonce()` overwrites the sender's `ethereumNonce` in the writable cache, bypassing nonce verification and allowing simulation of a transaction that would have been rejected by the consensus node, thereby producing a falsified historical execution result?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: patchSenderNonce()] Can an unprivileged external user set the nonce field inside `ethereumData` to a value far ahead of the account's actual `ethereumNonce` in the mirror node DB, causing `patchSenderNonce()` to inject a future nonce into the writable cache and simulate a transaction that never occurred in Hashgraph history, producing a fraudulent trace?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: patchSenderNonce()] Can an unprivileged external user set the nonce in `ethereumData` to zero (or any past value) to replay a previously executed historical transaction under a different nonce context, bypassing the nonce check that would normally prevent re-execution, and thereby manipulating the simulated Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: buildEthereumTransactionBody()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `ethereumData` with `maxGasAllowance` overridden to `Long.MAX_VALUE` (hardcoded) to simulate a historical Ethereum transaction with unlimited gas, producing an execution trace that is impossible to match against the actual on-chain record and thus constitutes a tampered history view?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: defaultTransactionBodyBuilder()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger `defaultTransactionBodyBuilder()` which uses `Instant.now()` as `transactionValidStart` instead of the historical consensus timestamp, causing the simulated transaction body to have a timestamp that never existed in Hashgraph history and producing a falsified historical replay?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: defaultTransactionBodyBuilder()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `transactionValidStart` is always set to `Instant.now()` (wall-clock time) rather than the historical block's consensus timestamp, so that a historical replay simulation uses a future/present timestamp, causing the EVM to evaluate time-dependent contract logic incorrectly and diverge from actual Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: buildContractCallTransactionBody()] Can an unprivileged external user supply arbitrary `callData` bytes via `params.getCallData()` to `buildContractCallTransactionBody()`, causing the executor to simulate a contract call with a completely different function selector and arguments than what was originally recorded in Hashgraph history, producing a falsified trace?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: buildContractCallTransactionBody()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a crafted `receiver` address (via `params.getReceiver().toArrayUnsafe()`) that differs from the actual historical contract address, causing `buildContractCallTransactionBody()` to simulate a call to a different contract than what was recorded in Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: buildContractCallTransactionBody()] Can an unprivileged external user set `params.getValue()` to an arbitrary tinybar amount in `buildContractCallTransactionBody()`, simulating a historical transaction with a different value transfer than what was actually recorded on Hashgraph, thereby producing a divergent execution trace?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: buildContractCreateTransactionBody()] Can an unprivileged external user supply arbitrary `initcode` via `params.getCallData()` to `buildContractCreateTransactionBody()`, causing the executor to simulate a contract deployment with different bytecode than what was originally deployed in Hashgraph history, producing a falsified creation trace?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: buildContractCreateTransactionBody()] Can an unprivileged external user pass an inflated `estimatedGas` value to `buildContractCreateTransactionBody()`, causing the simulated contract creation to succeed with more gas than was actually available at the historical block, producing a trace that diverges from actual Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSenderAccountID()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a sender address that resolves to a hollow account (no key or `IMMUTABILITY_SENTINEL_KEY`), causing `getSenderAccountID()` to inject `DEFAULT_KEY` into the writable cache and complete the hollow account, simulating a transaction that would have failed at the consensus node due to incomplete account state, thus tampering with historical execution?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSenderAccountID()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a sender address that maps to a smart contract account, causing `getSenderAccountID()` to silently substitute the treasury account as the payer, simulating a historical transaction with a different payer than what was recorded in Hashgraph, thereby producing a falsified execution context?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSenderAccountIDAsNum()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a long-zero EVM address that maps to a different `AccountID` than the actual historical sender, causing `getSenderAccountIDAsNum()` to resolve the wrong account and simulate the transaction under a different identity than what was recorded in Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSenderAccountIDAsNum()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an alias address that resolves via `aliasesReadableKVState.get()` to a different account than the actual historical sender, causing the simulation to execute under a wrong identity and produce a trace that diverges from actual Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: execute()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a `ContractDebugParameters` with `ethereumData` set to a valid RLP-encoded Ethereum transaction for a completely different historical transaction (different sender, receiver, value, data), causing `execute()` to replay the wrong transaction and produce a falsified opcode trace attributed to the wrong Hashgraph record?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: execute()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger `execute()` with a `ContractDebugParameters` whose `ethereumData` encodes a transaction with a different `chainId` than the network's actual chain ID, causing the executor to simulate a cross-chain transaction that never existed in Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: buildEthereumTransactionBody()] Can an unprivileged external user supply malformed or oversized `ethereumData` bytes to `buildEthereumTransactionBody()` that `populateEthTxData()` parses incorrectly, causing `patchSenderNonce()` to extract a wrong nonce and inject it into the writable cache, corrupting the simulated account state relative to actual Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: patchSenderNonce()] Can an unprivileged external user craft `ethereumData` where `populateEthTxData(params.getEthereumData()).nonce()` returns a negative or overflow value, causing `patchSenderNonce()` to write an invalid nonce into the writable cache and corrupt the account state used for historical simulation?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: patchSenderNonce()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the condition `params.getSender().isZero() && params.getValue() == 0L` in `patchSenderNonce()` to bypass the early return guard by providing a non-zero sender with zero value, ensuring the nonce patch always executes and overwrites the historical account nonce?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: handleFailedResult()] Can an unprivileged external user set an `OpcodeContext` in `ContractCallContext` before calling `execute()`, causing `handleFailedResult()` to return a failed `EvmTransactionResult` instead of throwing `MirrorEvmTransactionException`, thereby suppressing error information and allowing the caller to receive a manipulated result for a historically failed transaction?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: handleFailedResult()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the opcode context branch in `handleFailedResult()` to obtain a successful-looking `EvmTransactionResult` for a transaction that actually failed in Hashgraph history, by ensuring `ContractCallContext.get().getOpcodeContext() != null` during the call?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: buildSuccessResult()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a scenario where child transactions fail but the parent succeeds, causing `buildSuccessResult()` to return a `SUCCESS` result while silently logging child errors, allowing the caller to receive a misleadingly successful historical trace that hides actual Hashgraph sub-transaction failures?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/TransactionExecutionService.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: populateChildTransactionErrors()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a simulation where child transactions have status `REVERTED_SUCCESS`, causing `populateChildTransactionErrors()` to skip them (treating them as non-errors), and thus producing a historical trace that omits actual revert events recorded in Hashgraph?
